topic_definition: Captures JD language demanding low-level performance work (profiling, latency/throughput tuning, memory/CPU optimization), systems-internals depth (OS, kernel, networking, distributed-systems internals, hardware-aware programming), and explicit depth-claim framings ('deep understanding of X', 'expert-level', 'from first principles'). Distinct from high-level architecture/orchestration: this is the internals/low-level end where employers demand expertise that AI tooling cannot easily substitute.

topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude when followed by 'review/reviews/cycle/feedback/management'.

topic_exclusions.reason: HR term for managing employee performance.
topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude unless context contains technical terms (latency, throughput, system, code, application).
Exclude 'high[- ]performing team(s)' and 'high performance team(s)' / 'high-performance team(s)'.
Exclude 'high[- ]performance (culture|environment|workplace|organization)'.
Exclude 'KPI', 'KPIs', 'key performance indicator(s)'.
Exclude when 'performance' is preceded by sales/marketing/financial/business.
Exclude 'performance (bonus|incentive|pay|based pay|based compensation)'.
Only count 'profile/profiler/profiling' in compound technical contexts (CPU/memory/heap/code/perf).

topic_exclusions.pattern: user profile / customer profile / company profile
topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude when 'profile' is preceded by user/customer/company/candidate/applicant/buyer.
Exclude 'scale (your|our) (career|impact|skills|business)' unless followed by technical noun.

topic_exclusions.pattern: deep understanding of our customers / business / market
topic_exclusions.guard: For depth_claim_language, require a co-occurring technical noun within 5 tokens (system, kernel, distributed, OS, hardware, GPU, networking, database, compiler, algorithm, etc.). Otherwise drop or label as non-technical-depth.

topic_exclusions.reason: Generic discovery/analysis phrase, not depth claim.
topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude standalone 'deep dive(s)/deep diving' unless followed by a technical noun.

topic_exclusions.pattern: real estate / real time pay / real-time chat
topic_exclusions.guard: For 'real time/realtime', prefer co-occurrence with system/processing/streaming/data/inference/serving.
Word-boundary match; case sensitive optional.
Only count 'GC' in proximity to 'tuning/pause/heap/JVM/garbage/collector'.

topic_exclusions.reason: Acronym collision (e.g., 'OS' could be other things).
topic_exclusions.guard: Require 'OS internals/OS-level/operating system internals' rather than bare 'OS'.

topic_exclusions.pattern: SME (subject matter expert) vs SME (small and medium enterprise)
topic_exclusions.guard: Only count 'SME' when co-occurring with technical domain noun, or prefer the spelled-out 'subject matter expert'.
Generally accept; flag only if co-occurring with non-technical context.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'Raft' is also a generic noun (e.g., 'a raft of features').
topic_exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with consensus/replication/leader/distributed within ~10 tokens.

topic_exclusions.pattern: Triton (NVIDIA GPU lang) vs Triton (Inference Server) vs Triton (other product names)
topic_exclusions.reason: Multiple Triton products; mostly all relevant to perf.
topic_exclusions.guard: Generally accept; both senses indicate hardware/perf depth.
Treat 'fundamentals' as depth signal only when paired with technical domain noun (CS, OS, networking, distributed systems, etc.).

topic_calibration_recommendations: 1) Run a sample (n=200) of high-match postings and manually classify each as technical-perf vs HR-perf vs business-perf; the depth_claim_language and performance_optimization_general concepts will need the most filtering. 2) Implement co-occurrence guards for ambiguous tokens ('deep understanding of', 'real time', 'profile', 'GC', 'OS', 'scale') by requiring a technical noun within a 5-10 token window. 3) Treat depth_claim_language as a *modifier* concept: report it both raw and as 'depth_claim near technical noun' to separate fluff from genuine depth demand. 4) Track per-concept density (occurrences / 1000 tokens) rather than binary presence; this concept area will show most signal in the *intensity* dimension over 2024-2026. 5) Cross-check overlap with the Orchestration vocabulary: 'distributed systems' appears in both — decide whether to dual-count or assign primary topic by adjacent tokens (internals/consensus/replication => this list; architecture/design/microservices => Orchestration). 6) Validate hardware-aware terms (CUDA, FPGA, GPU) against role-title metadata — they spike in ML/infra roles and may need normalization across role cells.
topic_notes: This list emphasizes the *internals / low-level* end of the SWE skill spectrum, where the hypothesis is that AI substitution is weakest and JD demand may rise during 2024-2026. Three concepts (performance_optimization_general, depth_claim_language, scaling_and_efficiency) have the highest false-positive risk and should be reported with both raw counts and guarded counts. Compound forms ('performance-critical', 'low-latency') are listed both hyphenated and unhyphenated to handle inconsistent JD typography. Acronym-heavy concepts (CUDA, RDMA, DPDK, eBPF) are reliable signals — case-insensitive match recommended but consider preserving case-sensitive match for short acronyms that collide (BPF, GC, OS, SME). Depth-claim phrases are template-like; a windowed co-occurrence regex ('(deep|strong|expert|in-depth|thorough)\W+(understanding|knowledge|expertise|grasp|familiarity)\W+of\W+(\w+\W+){0,5}(<technical noun>)') will outperform raw-phrase matching.
